title: Finding gradient and y-intercept from two points on a graph
problem: A straight line passes through the points $(0, 2)$ and $(4, 10)$. Find the gradient and y-intercept of the line.

steps.instruction: Find the rise (vertical change) between the two points.
Find the run (horizontal change) between the two points.
Calculate the gradient using rise over run.
Read the y-intercept from the point where $x = 0$.
steps.working: The point $(0, 2)$ is on the y-axis, so the y-intercept is $2$.

title: Finding gradient from two points using the gradient formula
problem: Find the gradient of the line passing through $(2, 3)$ and $(5, 12)$.

steps.instruction: Label the two points as $(x_1, y_1)$ and $(x_2, y_2)$.
Substitute the coordinates into the formula.

title: Interpreting gradient as a rate of change in context
problem: A car travels at a constant speed. After 1 hour it has covered 60 km, and after 3 hours it has covered 180 km. Find the gradient of the distance-time graph and explain what it represents.

steps.instruction: Write the two data values as coordinate points $(\text{time}, \text{distance})$.

steps.working: The gradient is $60$. Since the axes are hours and kilometres, the gradient represents a rate of $60$ km per hour.

answer: Gradient $= 60$. It represents the speed of the car: $60$ km/h.
